content:
- Retry of the final required task-finished commit after the first attempt was interrupted by a transient stale git index lock.
- All changes are staged; if this commit lands cleanly the only remaining step is git push.

- Read prior progress log, `.ralph/current_tasks.md`, and `.ralph/current_tasks_done.md`.
- Opened every active candidate task file to inspect priority and blocker metadata directly.
- No active bug tasks were present in current tasks, and no candidate exposed `<blocked_by>` tags.
- Low-priority tasks in cluster startup and switchover stories were outranked by the two normal-priority managed-start-intent tasks.
- Chose `.ralph/tasks/story-managed-start-intent-architecture/task-remove-managed-conf-parseback-and-rederive-start-intent.md` as the most logical next task because it is the broader architectural correction and a better predecessor than the narrower typed-endpoints improvement.
- Wrote the selected path to `.ralph/current_task.txt` and should quit immediately.

- Read previous progress log and .ralph/current_task.txt.
- Current task path is .ralph/tasks/story-managed-start-intent-architecture/task-remove-managed-conf-parseback-and-rederive-start-intent.md.
- Opening the task file now to determine whether it is in planning, TO BE VERIFIED, or NOW EXECUTE state.

- Read the active task description and acceptance criteria.
- Determined the file is still in planning state; no TO BE VERIFIED or NOW EXECUTE marker yet.
- Inspecting concrete symbol usage across src/ so the plan can name exact files, helpers, and tests to change.

- Audited the active task and confirmed it was still in planning mode.
- Inspected the concrete parse-back chain in src/runtime/node.rs, src/ha/process_dispatch.rs, src/postgres_managed.rs, src/postgres_managed_conf.rs, and src/pginfo/conninfo.rs.
- Wrote a detailed execution plan into the active task file covering the audit, minimal local-fact replacement, runtime/HA changes, parser removal, test rewrites, docs updates, and final verification sequence.
- Added the required TO BE VERIFIED marker at the end of the plan and stopped before implementation.
- Next turn must do a deep skeptical review of the plan, alter at least one thing in it, and only then promote it to NOW EXECUTE.

- Read prior progress and confirmed the active task is task-remove-managed-conf-parseback-and-rederive-start-intent.md.
- The task is expected to be in TO BE VERIFIED phase from the previous turn.
- Opening the task file now for a skeptical review before any execution.

- Read the active task file in TO BE VERIFIED state and rechecked the concrete runtime/node.rs, ha/process_dispatch.rs, postgres_managed.rs, postgres_managed_conf.rs, and pginfo/conninfo.rs boundaries.
- Skeptical review changed the plan in a material way: parser deletion is now explicitly conditional on a repo-wide caller audit, rather than assumed; also added a shared minimal local-fact helper requirement for runtime and HA.
- Replaced TO BE VERIFIED with NOW EXECUTE after altering the plan as required.
- Stopping now per the workflow; the next turn should execute the written plan directly without further exploration.

- Executed the NOW EXECUTE plan: removed the managed conf parse-back path, added inspect_managed_recovery_state as the shared minimal local-fact helper, rewired runtime/node.rs and ha/process_dispatch.rs to rely on DCS plus local residue checks only, and deleted the managed conninfo/pg conninfo parser code and parser-only tests.
- Ran focused cargo tests for the new boundary: inspect_managed_recovery_state, start_postgres_dispatch, and select_resume_start_intent all passed.
- Next is the full required verification gate: make check, make test, make test-long, make lint; then update the task file with execution notes, set passes true, switch task, commit, and push.
